title: School zone speeding fines raise concerns about equity, spending priorities (Your Letters)
summary: A South Side community resident raises concerns about school zone speed enforcement equity, citing disproportionate impact on low-income families and perceived disparities across neighborhoods. The letter calls for transparent budgeting, clarification on fine revenue allocation, and consistent policy application to balance student safety with fairness.

political_signal.description: Community advocacy for policy review on school zone enforcement; raises concerns about equity in implementation and resource allocation transparency

processing_notes: Article is opinion/letters-to-editor; no specific enforcement data, dollar amounts, or incidents cited
Addresses systemic concerns rather than single incident; signals potential broader community sentiment on South Side
References vague geographic disparities ('predominantly non-white neighborhoods') without specific neighborhoods named
CIO relevance: transparency, public communication, and potential data systems needed to track enforcement equity across neighborhoods
